title: Google adds computer use to Gemini 3.5 Flash
friendlyTitle: Gemini can now use computer screens and apps more directly
summary: Google made computer use a built-in tool in Gemini 3.5 Flash, letting developers build agents that can see, reason, and act across browser, mobile, and desktop environments. Google highlights enterprise uses such as continuous software testing, knowledge-work automation, and accessibility audits, with safeguards for prompt-injection and sensitive actions.
friendlySummary: Google added computer-use abilities to Gemini 3.5 Flash. Developers can build AI agents that interact with websites, desktop apps, and mobile apps.
whyItMatters: Computer-use agents are becoming a standard model capability rather than a separate experimental add-on. For businesses, this makes the next wave of automation less about chat responses and more about safely operating real software with sandboxing, permissions, and human confirmation.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could help automate boring screen-based work, like testing software or checking documents. It also needs strong safety rules so the AI does not click the wrong big red button.
